subject: [LSF/MM/BPF TOPIC][RFC PATCH v4 00/27] Private Memory Nodes (w/ Compressed RAM)

reviews.author: Balbir Singh
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that slub.c injects __GFP_THISNODE internally on behalf of kmalloc, causing spillage into private nodes because slub allows private nodes in its mask, and suggested this is fixable.
reviews.raw_body:
That's good progress, thanks for the update!

Balbir

2026-06-11.patch_summary: This patch series introduces Private Memory Nodes, which allows for isolation of memory allocation on a per-node basis. The goal is to enable features like compressed RAM and improve system resource management. However, the patch also raises questions about how to limit page allocations to folio allocations or disallow slab allocations on private nodes. It appears that implementing such restrictions would be complex and may not provide significant benefits.
